1.solution:
Let rates: a‚â§b‚â§c. Slowest two: a+b=1/n. Quickest two: b+c=1/m. Middle person b alone = 3T (given one person takes 3√ó all-three time T).
‚Üí b=1/(3T). a=1/n‚àí1/(3T), c=1/m‚àí1/(3T).
‚Üí a+b+c = 1/n+1/m‚àí1/(3T) = 1/T ‚Üí 1/n+1/m = 4/(3T) ‚Üí T=4mn/(3(m+n)).
Answer: D (4mn/3(m+n)).

2.solution:
Rates: A=1/5, B=1/15, C=‚àí1/10. Per 3-day cycle (B,C,A): 1/15‚àí1/10+1/5=2/30‚àí3/30+6/30=5/30=1/6.
‚Üí After 5 cycles (15 days): 5/6 done. Remaining=1/6.
Day16 (B): +1/15 ‚Üí 27/30=9/10. Day17 (C): ‚àí1/10 ‚Üí 4/5. Day18 (A): +1/5 ‚Üí 1.
‚Üí Exactly 18 days. Not among options A/B/C.
Answer: D (None of these).

3.solution:
Let Jayant=4T, Ashok=2T, Prithvi=T, Prashant=(3/4)√ó4T=3T.
Prithvi+Jayant rate: 1/T+1/4T=5/4T ‚Üí time=4T/5.
Ashok+Prashant rate: 1/2T+1/3T=5/6T ‚Üí time=6T/5.
Ratio = (4T/5):(6T/5) = 4:6 = 2:3.
a+b = 2+3 = 5.
Answer: 5.

4.solution:
Given: (i) 2W+3M < 4C+1M ‚Üí W+M < 2C. (ii) 3W < 2C.
Statement I (M>C): Not determinable ‚Äî M can be >C or <C while satisfying (i). False/indeterminate.
Statement II (M+W < 2C): Directly from condition (i). Always true.
Statement III (M+W > 2C): Contradicts (i). Always false.
‚Üí Only Statement II is correct.
Answer: B (1).

5.solution:
Fill rate=1/6, drain rate=1/12. With f fills and d drains, n=f+d: f/6‚àíd/12=1/2 ‚Üí 2f‚àíd=6 ‚Üí 3f=n+6 ‚Üí f=(n+6)/3.
For valid integer solutions: n‚â°0 (mod 3) and d=n‚àíf=(2n‚àí6)/3‚â•0 ‚Üí n‚â•3.
Check: n=24‚Üíf=10,d=14 ‚úì; n=16‚Üí22/3 ‚úó; n=33‚Üíf=13,d=20 ‚úì; n=13‚Üí19/3 ‚úó; n=9‚Üíf=5,d=4 ‚úì; n=8‚Üí14/3 ‚úó.
Valid values: 24, 33, 9 ‚Üí 3 values.
Answer: 3.

6.solution:
Inlet rate=1/60/min. Outlet1(5m): half-tank in 90min ‚Üí rate=1/180/min. Outlet2(8m): 20% in 72min ‚Üí rate=1/360/min. Outlet3(9.5m): 5% in 18min ‚Üí rate=1/360/min.
Phase1 (0‚Üí50%, only inlet): 0.5/(1/60)=30 min.
Phase2 (50‚Üí80%, inlet‚àíoutlet1): net=1/60‚àí1/180=1/90. 0.3/(1/90)=27 min.
Phase3 (80‚Üí95%, inlet‚àíoutlet1‚àíoutlet2): net=1/60‚àí1/180‚àí1/360=3/360=1/120. 0.15/(1/120)=18 min.
Total=30+27+18=75 min.
Answer: 75.

7.solution:
Day 2k‚àí1: k men, (k‚àí1) women. Day 2k: k men, k women. 3W=2M ‚Üí W=2M/3.
Work day 2k‚àí1 = kM+(k‚àí1)(2M/3)=M(5k‚àí2)/3. Work day 2k = kM+k(2M/3)=5kM/3.
Total 40 days (k=1 to 20): M/3√óŒ£[(5k‚àí2)+5k]=M/3√óŒ£[10k‚àí2]=M/3√ó(2100‚àí40)=2060M/3.
30 women rate=30√ó(2M/3)=20M/day. Days=(2060M/3)/(20M)=2060/60‚âà34.33 ‚Üí nearest=34.
Answer: A (34).

8.solution:
Let Kumar=k days, Raja=k+5 days. Together t days, then Raja alone 5 days.
Raja's share: (t+5)/(k+5)=0.6. Kumar's share: t/k=0.4 ‚Üí t=0.4k.
Substitute: (0.4k+5)/(k+5)=0.6 ‚Üí 0.4k+5=0.6k+3 ‚Üí 0.2k=2 ‚Üí k=10.
Verify: t=4, Raja=(4+5)/15=60% ‚úì, Kumar=4/10=40% ‚úì.
Answer: 10.

9.solution:
n fill (rate 1/10) and p drain (rate 1/16): n/10‚àíp/16=1/4 ‚Üí 8n‚àí5p=20 ‚Üí p=(8n‚àí20)/5.
For integer p‚â•1: n must be multiple of 5 (n=5,10,15,20,25...).
‚Üí n+p values: n=5‚Üí9, n=10‚Üí22, n=15‚Üí35, n=20‚Üí48, n=25‚Üí61...
Check 36: 8n‚àí5(36‚àín)=20 ‚Üí 13n=200 ‚Üí n=15.4, not integer. So 36 is impossible.
Answer: B (36).

10.solution:
Worker1 always adds milk (odd turns). Worker2 pattern: milk,milk,water,milk,milk,water... (even turns).
Every 6 turns: Worker1 adds 3 milk, Worker2 adds 2 milk+1 water ‚Üí 5 milk, 1 water per 6 turns.
After 24 turns (4 cycles): 20 milk + 4 water = 24 cups.
Turn 25 (Worker1, milk): 21 milk + 4 water = 25 cups (half full).
Answer: D (21).
